A test case for the subtraction of string numbers in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that checks the behavior of the subtraction operator when applied to string operands in JavaScript. It verifies that subtracting the string `"1"` from another string `"1"` results in the number `0`. This test demonstrates JavaScript's type coercion, where the strings are implicitly converted to numbers before the subtraction operation. The code provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific aspect of JavaScript's type conversion and arithmetic operations.
